title: Container Threat Models, Users, Capabilities, Seccomp, Rootless, and Da… · Container Boundary Trace: Docker Seccomp Apparmor Selinux No New
summary: A third-party document converter handles hostile files but requests root, privileged mode, every device, and the Docker socket. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker seccomp apparmor selinux no new increment in least-privilege container threat model and hardening profile. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Retain or tighten seccomp, AppArmor or SELinux, no-new-privileges, read-only roots, masked paths, and writable tmpfs exceptions.
Start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and Docker socket access as host authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-seccomp-apparmor-selinux-no-new — Retain or tighten seccomp, AppArmor or SELinux, no-new-privileges, read-only roots, masked paths, and writable tmpfs exceptions.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-security-least-privilege-2
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-security-least-privilege-2
    artifact: security-policy.yaml
    decision: worked docker security least privilege 2
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      user: 10001:10001
      read_only: true
      cap_drop: [ALL]
      security_opt: [no-new-privileges:true]
      tmpfs: [/tmp]
      network_mode: none
    verify: docker inspect document-worker --format json
    failure: Reject privileged mode, daemon-socket mounting, broad devices, root-by-default, or a claim that containers form a hostile-code sandbox.
    repair: Remove daemon authority, choose rootless or user namespaces where applicable, drop capabilities, bound writable paths, and state residual kernel risk.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
